claim.content: Let a Slater-Jastrow-backflow trial wave function for a finite periodic simulation cell be parameterized by Jastrow and backflow parameters that were optimized by variational Monte Carlo (VMC) for the closed-shell ground state; denote these ground-state-optimized parameter vectors by \\((J_{\\mathrm{GS}},B_{\\mathrm{GS}})\\). The proposition is: when computing excitation energies in that finite simulation cell (energy differences between excited and ground states obtained by DMC), using the same fixed parameter values \\((J_{\\mathrm{GS}},B_{\\mathrm{GS}})\\) for the excited-state trial wave functions introduces less additional finite-size bias into the excitation energies than performing an independent reoptimization of \\(J,B\\) in each excited-state finite cell, because excited-state reoptimization in a finite cell can exploit finite-size-dependent changes in correlation that do not persist in the thermodynamic limit.
